title: Remove string interpolation from a fragment
prompt: `MyApp.Blog.Search.find/1` in `test_fixtures/fragment_interpolation_bug.ex` interpolates `user_input` directly into a fragment literal with `#{}` — SQL injection. Fix it using placeholder-style fragments: `fragment("? ILIKE ?", p.body, ^pattern)` with the pattern built outside the fragment. Do the same for `by_score_threshold/1` (replace it with a typed comparison). Write the fix to `lib/my_app/blog/search.ex`.
tier_rationale: Well-known security rule; straightforward substitution of `#{}` with `?` placeholders. Ecto also raises a compile error for `#{}` inside fragment, so the fix direction is unambiguous.
research_citation: Victor Bjorklund: 'fragment does not allow strings to be interpolated as the first argument via the ^ operator. You must use placeholders (?)'
